text:
Admission Date:  2009-07-16              Discharge Date:   2009-07-16

Date of Birth:  1987-01-20             Sex:   F

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Andrew
Chief Complaint:
Overdose


Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
None

History of Present Illness:
HPI: 21 yo female s/p excedrin OD (05-13 bottle) and ETOH.  She
says she was angry at self after doing poorly in class at
school, but also had a headache.  She had 02-17 drinks at a bar
over about 5 hours, then went home and took pills.  She is
unclear exactly how many pills she took - estimates less than
50, closer to 20.  She has no SOB, no CP, no ringing in ears, no
HA, no fever/chills.  After took pills, developed nausea and
emesis x2, nonbloody.  During the headache she had no
photophobia, no phonophobia, and no fevers.  She denies trying
to commit suicide, says she just took too many pills.  No h/o
depression, no h/o suicidal ideation, no other stressors other
than school.  Denies abdominal pain, shortness of breath, chest
pressure.
.
In the ED she was given activated charcoal and Anzemet.
.


Past Medical History:
PMHx: None, no h/o depression

Social History:
Social history: Student at [**University/College **] - senior, lives in house with 60
women, majoring in chemical engineering and biology, plans to
work in a lab for a year and then apply to med school.  + EtOH
about 1-2x/week, no more than 02-17 drinks/night.  No tobacco, no
recreational drug use.  Family all in [**State 5887**].  Does not
want us to call her mother.

Family History:
Family history: No psychiatric history

Brief Hospital Course:
Assessment: 21 year old female s/p excedrin OD and EtOH usage.
.
#  tylenol OD - level 59.6 on arrival to ED at 3:30 AM,
decreased to 32.5 at 5:30 AM (the 4 hour mark for Rumack-Sarah
nomogram).  Did not meet criteria for N-acetylcysteine.  Patient
received activated charcoal in the ED. Tylenol levels are
trending down, peak was 59.6 (200 at 4 hour mark is borderline
for needing N-acetylcysteine).  LFTs were not elevated.
Toxicology was consulted and agreed with above.
.
# Leukocytosis:  With elevated white blood count, chest xray was
clear, urine culture was clear. No focal indications of
infection except mild productive cough.
.
#  aspirin OD - The patient did not have tinnitus, vertigo, or
fever.  In fact, UpToDate quotes 20mg/dL to be the therapeutic
dose, pt's level was 25mg/dL, so do not expect any toxicity from
this.  Defered aggressive alkalinization of urine.
.
#  EtOH - low likelihood of frank withdrawal.  Pt no longer
appears intoxicated.  Cont to monitor.
.
#  metabolic acidosis - Initial gap was 18, delta/delta close to
1.  Gap acidosis from lactate (3.3), aspirin, and EtOH.
Lactates were monitored and trended down at time of transfer.
.
# Psych- Psych was consulted for suicide attempt. [**University/College **] requires
psychiatric clearance prior to readmittance to [**University/College **], will be
transferred to Kevin for further assessment
.
#  FEN/GI - She was maintained on clear liquids for now, she was
slightly nauseated thus diet was not advanced.
.
#  Access - PIV
.
#  Code - full
.


Medications on Admission:
None

Discharge Medications:
1. Dolasetron Mesylate 12.5 mg IV Q8H:PRN nausea
2. Prochlorperazine 10 mg IV Q6H:PRN
3. Promethazine HCl 25 mg IV Q6H:PRN


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Va Medical Center - Syracuse Hospital - 8128 Brittany Shoals
Reedfurt, CA 63104

Discharge Diagnosis:
Drug Overdose


Discharge Condition:
Good


Discharge Instructions:
If you experience increased pain, shortness of breath, ringing
in your ears, please contact your doctor.

Please take your medications as instructed

Followup Instructions:
Transfer to Kevin
